domain: Progressive Difficulty & Scaffolding in Game-Based Learning
purpose: Inform Learning Looper level progression design

sources.title: Effects of Adaptive Scaffolding on Performance, Cognitive Load and Engagement in Game-Based Learning (Randomized Controlled Trial)
Interactive Effects of Scaffolding Digital Game-Based Learning and Cognitive Style on Adult Learners
A Review of Scaffolding Approaches in Game-based Learning Environments
Zone of Proximal Development in Game Design
Pathways to Mastery: A Taxonomy of Player Progression Systems in Commercial Video Games

cognitive_load_theory.description: Working memory has limited capacity. When cognitive load exceeds capacity, learning is hindered (cognitive overload). Well-designed progression reduces extraneous load by sequencing content.
cognitive_load_theory.implication: Introduce one class and one fact at a time. Never present all 6 classes × 15 attributes simultaneously.

zone_of_proximal_development.description: Vygotsky's ZPD: the gap between what a learner can do independently and what they can achieve with support. Optimal learning happens at the edge of current ability.
zone_of_proximal_development.evidence: Meta-analysis (Van de Pol et al., 2010): contingent scaffolding adjusted to learner's ZPD is 2.5x more effective than fixed support.
zone_of_proximal_development.implication: Difficulty must adapt to demonstrated mastery. If learner is getting 90%+, advance. If below 60%, hold or simplify.

mastery_based_gating.description: Progressive game levels that require demonstrated competence before unlocking new content. The progression system itself acts as scaffolding.
mastery_based_gating.evidence: Studies show mastery-gated progression increases use of learning material and improves outcomes vs. open-access designs.
mastery_based_gating.implication: Require ~80% accuracy on current level before unlocking next. Never skip ahead.

adaptive_scaffolding.description: Dynamically adjusting difficulty based on real-time performance. Decreases extraneous load when learner is overloaded; increases germane load when underloaded.
adaptive_scaffolding.evidence: Adaptive scaffolding improved speed, reduced need for self-regulation, and lowered perceived cognitive load vs. fixed scaffolding.
adaptive_scaffolding.implication: Track per-question accuracy in real-time. If streak breaks, reduce difficulty. If streak builds, advance.

progression_model.description: Proposed level structure for airspace quiz game based on research

levels.title: Class B Basics
levels.description: Learn one fact about one class. Binary choice: 'Class B entry requires ___ (clearance / radio contact)?'

levels.title: Class B Expanded
levels.description: Add more facts about Class B. Multiple choice from 3-4 options.

levels.title: B vs C
levels.description: Introduce Class C. Compare and contrast with B. 'Which class uses magenta lines?'

levels.title: Adding Class D
levels.description: Add Class D. Introduce transponder requirements. Compare D's dashed blue vs B's solid blue.

levels.title: Controlled Airspace
levels.description: All controlled classes. Sort attributes into correct class buckets.

levels.title: E and G
levels.description: Introduce uncontrolled and the 'everything else' class. Focus on what's NOT required.

levels.title: Full Picture
levels.description: All classes, all attributes. Mixed question formats. Timed optional.

levels.title: Boss Round
levels.description: Speed round. All content. Streak multipliers. Variable bonuses.
